commit.message:
feat: monster case stats, collection tracker & live feed (#235)

* feat: add stats, collection tracking, and live feed to monster case simulator

- Persist openings to localStorage (works immediately) and to Supabase
  via API (works after migration is applied)
- Stats tab: rarity breakdown bars with expected-rate markers, collection
  grid with silhouettes for missing items, recent drops history
- Live feed: polls /api/monster/feed for global drops from all users,
  gracefully hidden when DB migration hasn't been run yet
- Supabase migration: monster_opening table with RLS policies
- i18n: Norwegian + English translations for all new UI

* fix: address review feedback on monster stats PR

- Wrap localStorage.setItem in try/catch for private browsing/quota
- Use static Tailwind class maps instead of runtime string interpolation
- Use i18n translations for rarity labels instead of hardcoded English
- Precompute item counts in a single pass instead of O(variants × opens)
- Fix NaN limit param in feed route
- Use left join instead of inner join for profiles in feed query
- Validate item names against known variants in open route
- Single FK to profiles(id) instead of dual FK to auth.users + profiles
- Make migration idempotent (if not exists on indexes, exception handling
  on constraints)

* style: reformat all files with prettier (4-space indent)

CI uses prettier, not biome. Reformats all new and modified files to
match the project's prettier config (4-space indent, printWidth 100).

* feat: server-authoritative roll with rate limiting and stats aggregation

Move roll logic server-side (node:crypto.randomInt) so the client cannot
influence outcomes. Add 1s rate limit via last-opened_at query, auth
checks on all routes, and a Postgres aggregation view with
security_invoker for RLS-safe stats. Delete client-side recordOpening
(replaced by /api/monster/open). Unify rarity probabilities and case
catalog into a shared catalog.ts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add route-handler and roll tests for monster API

25 tests across 4 suites: roll distribution + catalog checks (6),
open route auth/rate-limit/validation (9), feed route auth + limit
clamping (6), stats route auth + aggregation (4). All green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: redesign monster page with 3-column layout and theme colors

Replace tab-based open/stats toggle with a 3-column desktop layout:
global stats + live feed on the left, roller in the center, personal
stats + collection + recent drops on the right. Stacks vertically on
mobile.

Key changes:
- Use app theme tokens (bg-surface, border-border, text-foreground)
  instead of hardcoded grays
- Split StatsPanel into StatsGlobal and StatsPersonal components
- Delay query invalidation until spin animation finishes to avoid
  spoiling the result in the side panels
- Result card appears between roller and button (no overlap)
- Add column headers ("Global Stats" / "Your Stats") for clarity
- Max-height + scroll on live feed and recent drops lists
- Make expected-rate marker amber for visibility
- Delete useOpeningHistory (replaced by server-side tracking)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: polish stats layout and hide Supabase errors from client

- Widen rarity label column so "Legendarisk" doesn't overflow into bar
- Align roller with side column tops via top padding
- Remove center column title (redundant with case selector)
- Return generic "server_error" to client, log Supabase details server-side
- Update expected-rate marker text (now gold, not white)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: adjust column widths in StatsPanel for better alignment and readability

- Shortened rarity label column to prevent excessive spacing
- Widened percentage column to avoid truncation of statistics

* lint

* fix: address copilot review feedback on monster stats PR

- feed/route: Math.floor on limit so non-integer query params can't reach .limit()
- StatsPanel: drop hardcoded "Expected:" tooltip; the i18n marker line below covers it
- StatsPanel: reuse RARITY_PROBABILITIES from catalog instead of duplicating EXPECTED_RATES
- page: surface generic open errors with new monster.openFailed key (en + no)
- stats/route: query new monster_opening_owned_items view instead of pulling all rows into Node
- migration: add (user_id, opened_at desc) and (user_id, case_type, opened_at desc) indexes for rate-limit and recent-drops hot paths; drop redundant idx_mo_user_case
- migration: add monster_opening_owned_items view (security_invoker, distinct user/case/item)
- StatsGlobal: hide entirely when stats endpoint is unavailable, matching LiveFeed and PR description

* Update apps/web/src/app/[locale]/(protected)/monster/components/LiveFeed.tsx

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: improve accessibility for unowned collection items

Use empty alt text and remove "???" tooltip for unowned variants so
screen readers don't announce confusing punctuation.

commit.message:
feat: Webhook event subscriptions for space write events (#60)

* Initial plan

* feat: add webhook subscription system for space write events

- Add webhook types, store, and dispatcher modules (server/src/webhooks/)
- Add admin API routes for webhook CRUD (POST/GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/admin/webhooks)
- Add delivery log endpoint (GET /api/admin/webhooks/:id/deliveries)
- Add test ping endpoint (POST /api/admin/webhooks/:id/test)
- Emit webhook events from brain API (memory, entity, edge, chrono CRUD)
- Emit webhook events from files API (file create, delete, move/rename)
- HMAC-SHA256 signing with shared secret for payload verification
- At-least-once delivery with exponential backoff retries
- HTTPS-only webhook URLs enforced via Zod validation
- Secret never returned in GET responses (write-once)

commit.message:
Fix DIFF tab: resolve workspace path + improve no-diff UX

- _ChangedFileTile now accepts explicit workspacePath param so it doesn't
  depend on WorkspaceCubit.activeWorkspace (which can be null); falls back
  to WorkspaceCubit only if explicit path not supplied
- _GitChangesSection passes state.fileTree.first.path as the workspace
  path, guaranteeing the right git root is used for diff lookup
- FileEditorCubit.openDiff: when git diff returns no hunks (untracked or
  binary file), falls back to reading file content via readAsString; binary
  files (readAsString throws) get an empty-hunks diff tab with 'No diff'
- _DiffBody: replaced invisible 'No changes' (dark-on-dark textMuted) with
  a visible 'No diff available' icon + message + filename
- Updated widget test to match new 'No diff available' text

commit.message:
fix(benchmarks): run defaults_tp on CUDA and save progress

Forward device and eval_batch_size through the defaults_tp suite into
feed-forward NEF benchmarks so Colab runs actually use the requested
CUDA device instead of silently staying on CPU.

Move single-layer and multi-layer benchmark models and tensors onto the
resolved runtime device, synchronize CUDA around fit timing, and evaluate
accuracy in batches to keep GPU memory bounded.

Persist suite JSON and CSV outputs after every successful benchmark step
so long Colab sweeps keep partial results when interrupted.

Share device helpers from benchmarks.run with the recurrent harness and
fix hybrid incremental initialization so temporary NEF layers are created
on the active device.

Motivation: defaults_tp on Colab was slower than expected because the
suite ignored --device, and interrupted runs lost all completed results.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Disable submit button until password meets all requirements
- Add ApiException class that parses error details from API responses

* fix: properly handle Keycloak error responses during user creation

- KeycloakAdminService.CreateUserAsync now returns KeycloakOperationResult
  with status code and parsed error message instead of just null on failure
- CreateUser endpoint maps Keycloak 409 to Conflict, 4xx to BadRequest with
  meaningful error details, and 5xx to 502 BadGateway
- Parses Keycloak JSON error responses (errorMessage, error_description, error)
  to surface human-readable messages to the frontend
- Prevents Aspire retry policy from retrying 400-level validation errors
  (which was causing the 3-attempt 500 pattern in logs)

* fix: pass Keycloak admin password to API and improve token error handling

The AppHost was passing the admin username but not the password to the API
project. Since Aspire generates a random admin password for Keycloak, the
KeycloakAdminService defaulted to 'admin' which was wrong, causing 401s.

Now reads the password from KeycloakResource.AdminPasswordParameter so the
API always gets the correct generated password.

Also improved EnsureAccessTokenAsync to log the actual error response body
before throwing, making auth failures diagnosable.

commit.message:
fix: race condition on track completion — old track re-streamed

Two bugs caused the wrong track to stream after auto-advance:

1. After _signal_track_completion sent update_playing_status(progress=end),
   the Ynison echo triggered false seek detection (drift ~2s), prematurely
   waking get_audio_stream before the actual index change arrived.
   Fix: update _last_progress_ms/time BEFORE sending so echo drift ≈ 0.

2. The premature wake read current_track_id from stale state (old track),
   streamed it at seek=end, then the real track change set seek=248s on
   the NEW track (from the echo of the second update_playing_status).
   Fix: extract _wait_for_track_change() that loops until current_track_id
   actually differs, ignoring same-track echoes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Balance pass: weighted monsters, tavern rest, economy tuning

- Monster spawning now uses dungeon.floor + weighted selection
  (native-floor monsters are most likely; danger scales correctly floor-to-floor)
- Reduced all monster stats slightly for fairer early game
- Skeleton, Zombie, etc. still present but weighted low on floor 1
- Tavern now offers: Rest for 10gp → roll 1d4 HP (gamble)
- Healer gains 'Heal 1 HP (5gp)' button alongside full-heal option
- Starting gold increased 10gp → 25gp (afford a rest or small heal early)

commit.message:
Improve source generator incrementality with per-record outputs

Split the single .Collect()-based RegisterSourceOutput into two:
- Per-record output: emits each record's .g.cs independently, only
  re-runs when that specific record's AnalysisResult changes.
- Registry output: uses .Collect() but only emits RecordRegistry.g.cs.

This means changing one record no longer invalidates all other
generated record files. The AnalysisResult/RecordMetadata/FieldMetadata
types already implement IEquatable for proper caching.

Also changed EmitRegistry to accept IEnumerable<RecordMetadata>
instead of ImmutableArray<RecordMetadata> since the caller now
filters from the collected results.

commit.message:
feat: add Day/Week/Month period toggle to chart view (#594)

* feat: add Day/Week/Month period toggle to chart view

- Add ChartPeriodData interface and periods field to ChartDataPayload in types.ts
- Rewrite calculateDailyStats() to collect up to 365 days using consistent pipeline
- Rewrite buildChartData() to compute daily (30 days), weekly (6 weeks), and monthly (12 months) buckets
- Add lastFullDailyStats field to CopilotTokenTracker for full-year data caching
- Add period toggle buttons (Day/Week/Month) to chart webview
- Update summary cards to be period-aware (labels, count, avg)
- Preserve selected period across background chart updates

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: eliminate redundant getSessionFileDetails call in calculateDailyStats

Remove the duplicate statSessionFile + cache lookup that was happening for
every session file in the 365-day window. The SessionFileCache returned by
getSessionFileDataCached() already stores lastInteraction, so calling
getSessionFileDetails() on top was doing double the I/O work per file.

Also pre-warm full-year chart data in background after updateTokenStats()
so the chart panel opens instantly instead of computing 365 days on demand.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: open chart panel immediately; load week/month data in background

Previously the chart tab appeared empty for ~60s because calculateDailyStats()
(365-day scan) was awaited before createWebviewPanel() was even called.

Now the panel opens instantly with the 30-day data already in memory.
Week and Month toggle buttons show a loading indicator (⌛) and are
disabled while the full-year data is computed in the background. Once
ready, an updateChartData message enables them with correct data.

Also adds periodsReady flag to ChartDataPayload / InitialChartData and
CSS for disabled toggle buttons.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct period key mismatch causing chart to show black panel

The periods object in buildChartData() used keys 'daily/weekly/monthly'
but getActivePeriodData() looked up data.periods[currentPeriod] where
currentPeriod is 'day'/'week'/'month'. The lookup always returned
undefined, causing a TypeError crash in renderLayout() which was
silently swallowed by void bootstrap(), leaving the root div empty
and the webview panel black.

Fix: rename keys in ChartDataPayload (types.ts), buildChartData()
(extension.ts), and InitialChartData (webview/chart/main.ts) to use
the short form 'day'/'week'/'month' to match ChartPeriod.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
